approval_notes: Owner approved starting the F003 epic and, when presented with the scoped plan, selected 'Approve as scoped (implement now)' including the addition of the lightweight pyyaml==6.0.3 runtime dependency.

This is the FIRST of three F003 slices. Slice 1 = the configuration system (CODEX task 003 = F003 acceptance step 1: validate good and bad YAML with actionable errors). Slice 2 = the domain model + Alembic migrations (task 004 = acceptance steps 2 and 3). Slice 3 = the Z0 classification engine (task 005 = acceptance step 4).

F003 stays passes:false after this slice. Flipping F003 passes:true requires the FULL epic (config + domain model + Z0 engine) plus a fresh-context full-epic Level 2 evaluation, exactly as F002 was closed out.
objective: Add a typed, validated, documented declarative YAML configuration system: Pydantic v2 models for the four configuration families (application, schedules, llm-providers, provider files) that forbid unknown fields, a loader that produces actionable file-scoped errors for malformed YAML / unknown fields / missing required fields / inline secrets, a JSON Schema export, and a validation CLI plus cross-platform wrapper scripts. Secrets are referenced by environment-variable name only and never stored or printed.
scope: New package apps/api/app/config/: models.py (typed Pydantic v2 models, extra=forbid, closed Z0-class vocabulary from docs/DATA_MODEL.md, slug-validated open vocabularies, env-var-name references), loader.py (YAML parse + family detection + inline-secret scan + actionable error formatting), cli.py (validate + emit-schema), __init__.py.
scripts/validate-config.ps1 and scripts/validate-config.sh wrappers, invoked from scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/test.sh after pytest.
Add pyyaml==6.0.3 to pyproject.toml [project.dependencies] and apps/api/requirements.txt (kept in sync per tests/unit/test_requirements_sync.py; the worker subset is unaffected).

tests/unit/test_config_system.py: valid examples pass; unknown field, malformed YAML, missing required, bad cron, threshold ordering, mcp-without-capabilities, inline secret, and non-env-name reference all rejected with actionable errors; JSON Schema export; CLI success and failure paths.
docs/CONFIGURATION.md documenting the families, the validation commands, and the secret-name rule.
out_of_scope: The F003 domain model (Provider/Service/Offer/Quota/Region/Evidence/Snapshot/OfferVersion/ChangeEvent/ScanRun/ReviewItem), Alembic migrations, rollback/reapply, immutable offer versions, and evidence provenance -> F003 slice 2.
The Z0 classification engine and truth table (including unknown/contradictory handling) -> F003 slice 3.
Runtime consumption of this configuration by the API/worker/scheduler beyond validation; full secret injection wiring; provider adapters; any network or source fetching.
Marking F003 (or any feature) passes:true; adding managed-cloud services or heavyweight dependencies; advancing past this slice.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/validate-config.{ps1,sh} validates every *.yaml under config/examples (application, schedules, llm-providers, providers/cloudflare) and exits 0.
Each configuration family has a typed Pydantic model that forbids unknown fields; an unknown field produces an actionable error naming the file and the field path with a non-zero exit.
Malformed YAML produces an actionable error naming the file with the line and column of the syntax error, with a non-zero exit.
A missing required field produces an actionable error naming the field path (type=missing), with a non-zero exit.
python -m app.config.cli emit-schema <family> prints a valid JSON Schema (type=object with properties) for each of the four families.

Secret safety: a raw inline secret key (api_key, password, token, secret, etc.) anywhere in a file is rejected; secrets are referenced only via *_env environment-variable names; a non-env-name reference is rejected; the detect-secrets scan stays green and no secret value is printed.
The offline pytest suite (valid examples, unknown/malformed/missing/bad-cron/threshold/mcp/inline-secret/env-name, schema export, CLI) passes.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 (pytest + config validation + web tests/build) and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, secret scan, pip-audit, npm audit), including the requirements-sync guard with the new pyyaml pin.
No unsupported free-tier/zero-cost/Z0 product claim is introduced (backend configuration only; product-truth rule).
A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict in agent-state/evaluation.json before any status change is claimed; F003 remains passes:false (epic incomplete).

end_to_end_steps: Run scripts/validate-config.ps1 and confirm all four example configs validate with exit 0.
Craft an invalid config for each failure mode (unknown field, malformed YAML, missing required, inline secret) and confirm the CLI reports an actionable, file-scoped error and exits non-zero.
Run python -m app.config.cli emit-schema for each family and confirm a valid JSON Schema is printed.
Run scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm both pass end-to-end, including the requirements-sync guard.
Confirm the detect-secrets scan is green and no secret value appears in any config, code, or output.
negative_tests: A config containing a raw api_key (rather than api_key_env) is rejected with an 'inline secret' message.
A cron expression that is not five fields is rejected; a provider whose automatic_threshold is below uncertain_threshold is rejected; an mcp source without capabilities is rejected.
An unrecognised top-level structure is rejected with a family-detection error rather than being silently accepted.

risks.data_migration: No schema or migration change in this slice; configuration is parsed and validated only, not persisted.
risks.security_privacy: Secrets are referenced by environment-variable name only; the loader never reads or prints secret values and actively rejects inline secrets. detect-secrets must stay green.
risks.portability: pyyaml is a pure, ubiquitous, permissively licensed YAML parser (ADR 0004 mandates YAML config); it adds no managed-cloud dependency and builds on amd64 and arm64.
risks.zero_cost_safety: Backend configuration only; introduces no product claim. The closed Z0-class vocabulary mirrors docs/DATA_MODEL.md.
risks.regression: Adding pyyaml touches both pyproject.toml and apps/api/requirements.txt; the requirements-sync guard is re-run to confirm no drift.
risks.supply_chain: pyyaml==6.0.3 is an established release well beyond any quarantine window; no new npm dependency is added by this Python-only slice.

notes: Owner approved the scoped slice and the pyyaml dependency ('Approve as scoped (implement now)'). This slice does not mark F003 passing; it is the first of three F003 slices and requires an independent Level 2 evaluation before its own completion is claimed.
